text.title: quality-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for quality-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent writes assertion-free tests to hit the coverage percentage target.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Coverage tools measure lines executed, not assertion correctness. Gate on mutation score (mutmut, stryker) for critical modules to detect tautological tests.

failure-mode.description: Defect severity consistently classified as "Medium" by the triager.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Medium is the safest answer for an LLM avoiding both over- and under-escalation. Calibrate with rubric examples and force evidence citation per severity choice.

failure-mode.description: Agent auto-skips "n/a" DoD items without human approval.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: N/a bypass is the most exploited DoD escape hatch; require explicit human approval for any n/a tag to preserve the gate's integrity.

failure-mode.description: Static analyzer run produces 10,000 legacy issues; agent attempts to fix all in one PR.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bulk fixes in a single PR are impossible to review and routinely introduce regressions. Chunk by file or module with incremental PRs.
